title: Semiconductor devices and methods of manufacture thereof
abstract: Semiconductor devices and methods of manufacture thereof are disclosed. In some embodiments, a semiconductor device includes a first semiconductor chip including a first substrate and a first conductive feature formed over the first substrate, and a second semiconductor chip bonded to the first semiconductor chip. The second semiconductor chip includes a second substrate and a second conductive feature formed over the second substrate. A conductive plug is disposed through the first conductive feature and is coupled to the second conductive feature. The conductive plug includes a first portion disposed over the first conductive feature, the first portion having a first width, and a second portion disposed beneath or within the first conductive feature. The second portion has a second width. The first width is greater than the second width.
claims: 7. A method, comprising: directly bonding a first semiconductor chip to a second semiconductor chip, wherein a first electrically conductive feature of the first semiconductor chip is directly bonded to a second electrically conductive feature of the second semiconductor chip, wherein the first electrically conductive feature is disposed in a first insulating bonding layer, wherein a first portion of the first insulating bonding layer is disposed in a first opening extending through the first electrically conductive feature, and wherein directly bonding the first semiconductor chip to the second semiconductor chip comprises directly contacting the first portion of the first insulating bonding layer in the first opening with the second electrically conductive feature; forming a conductive plug extending through the first semiconductor chip, the conductive plug having: a first width in a first semiconductor substrate of the first semiconductor chip; a second width extending from the first semiconductor substrate into a top portion of the first electrically conductive feature, the second width being less than the first width, and the second width being measured from a first sidewall of the top portion of the first electrically conductive feature to a second sidewall of the top portion of the first electrically conductive feature; and a third width in a bottom portion of the first electrically conductive feature, the third width being less than the second width, the third width being measured from a third sidewall of the bottom portion of the first electrically conductive feature to a fourth sidewall of the bottom portion of the first electrically conductive feature.
